title: Shut-off criteria in `summary.json`
source: maxdama.pdf §4.10 alpha checklist + r/quant "This job is insane" thread (alpha decay reality check, cached at [cache/reddit/digest.md](cache/reddit/digest.md))
why: The pipeline has no explicit decommissioning rule. Real practitioners set "if 6-fold rolling median Sharpe < X, retire this primary" — without it, a primary that decays silently keeps consuming runtime and capital allocation. Per the r/quant thread: *"Found 1 alpha after researching for 3 years. Made small amount of money in live for 3 months with good sharpe. Alpha now looks decayed after just 3 months."* Alpha decay is the modal outcome, not a rare event.
value_rationale: M (downgraded from H in 2026-05-27 recalibration — Paso 2 prereq, but no audit-passed primary exists yet for shutoff to matter; B0040/B0032 unblock comes first)
scope: extend [pipeline/reporting.py](pipeline/reporting.py) + `summary.json` schema with a `shutoff` block: `{rolling_window, threshold, current_rolling_sharpe, status}`. Configurable threshold in [configs/xau_d1.yaml](configs/xau_d1.yaml). No automatic action — just reports the boolean.

history.reason: Concrete self-contained reporting addition (maxdama 4.10 decommissioning rule). Picked over B0010 (gated on empirical evidence B0032 patch is insufficient) — B0010 premature until an H4 audit runs with the new cv-cap.

Decommissioning rule (maxdama 4.10). compute_shutoff_status in pipeline/reporting.py: nan-median of best-model fold Sharpe over last rolling_window folds vs threshold -> status ACTIVE/RETIRE/INSUFFICIENT_DATA. Reported only, no automatic action. Config block shutoff{rolling_window:6, threshold:0.0} in configs/xau_d1.yaml. Wired into summary.json[shutoff] + a report.md section. Tests: tests/test_shutoff_status.py (8: active/retire, window tail, nan-skip, all-nan insufficient, equality boundary=active, positive-threshold retire, window>history). 570/570 pass.
feat(B0002): decommissioning rule (shutoff) in summary.json + report.md
